# Introduction to Earth Observation 
(EO) and its applications in development projects

## Objectives
By the end of this module, you will be able to:
- Understand the basics of Earth Observation (EO) and its applications in development projects.
- Learn about different EO data sources and how to access them.
- Identify tools and techniques for analyzing EO data.

## Prerequisites
None

## Important Links
[Satellite State of Play](https://landscape.satsummit.io/)  
[Online course: Enhancing FCV Operations with Geospatial ICT tools](https://wbg.edcast.com/insights/enhancing-fcv-operations-with-geospatial-ict-tools)  
[Course blog](https://blogs.worldbank.org/opendata/enhancing-fcv-operations-geospatial-ict-tools-new-world-bank-online-course-explores-use)  
[Google earth engine tutorials](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/tutorials/community/satellite-embedding-03-supervised-classification)  
[Info on band combinations](https://eos.com/make-an-analysis/)  
[Sentinel-2 band combinations](https://gisgeography.com/sentinel-2-bands-combinations/)  

| Course | Details |
| ----- | ----- |
| Instructors | Katie L. McWilliams and Clara Ivanescu |
| Duration | 2-3 hours |
| Level | Beginner |
| Software | Python |
